title: Child deaths in the first five years of life by age group
subtitle: Number of children who died before reaching the age of five. Shown by the time period in which they died.
caption:
Child deaths in the first five years of life by age group

Number of children who died before reaching the age of five. Shown by the time period in which they died.

This line chart tracks the global number of child deaths occurring within the first five years of life, categorized by specific age groups, from 1980 to 2023. The vertical axis measures the number of deaths in millions, ranging from 0 to 5 million, while the horizontal axis marks the years. Four distinct lines represent different age cohorts: 0-6 days, 7-27 days, 28-364 days, and 1-4 years. In 1980, the 28-364 days group recorded the highest number of deaths, starting at approximately 4.6 million, followed closely by the 1-4 years group at roughly 4.4 million. The 0-6 days group began at about 3.6 million, and the 7-27 days group was the lowest at approximately 1.3 million. Over the observed period, all four groups exhibit a consistent downward trend. By 2023, the values have decreased significantly across all categories. The 0-6 days group ends at approximately 1.7 million, becoming the category with the highest number of deaths in the most recent year. The 28-364 days and 1-4 years groups converge near the bottom of the chart, finishing around 1.3 million and 1.2 million respectively. The 7-27 days group remains the lowest throughout the timeline, ending just above 0.4 million. The lines for the older age groups cross below the neonatal (0-6 days) line around the year 2008, indicating a shift in the distribution of mortality by age.
